frameworks.name: attentional blink (AB)
frameworks.one_line: second-target deficit within ~500 ms indexes capacity-limited attentional selection

frameworks.name: post-perceptual bottleneck account
frameworks.one_line: claim that AB spares perception and only disrupts working-memory consolidation

frameworks.name: working memory
frameworks.one_line: downstream store whose access differs across feature levels during the blink

core_question: Does the attentional blink disrupt perceptual processing, or only post-perceptual consolidation?
key_assumptions: if perception is intact, all features of T2 should access working memory equally
report-feature manipulation isolates the level at which the blink acts
baseline performance can be equated across report conditions
manipulations: reporting feature of T2 (identity vs semantic category vs color)
T1-T2 lag to traverse the AB depth
key_findings: identity and semantic-category reports were more impaired by the blink than color reports
baseline (no-blink) performance was matched across report conditions
high-level features are more poorly represented in WM than low-level features during the blink
interpretations: the attentional blink disrupts perceptual processing, not only post-perceptual stages
